_note: Operational change log — vendor breakages, deprecations, rate-limit changes, migration deadlines. Hand-curated. Each item carries a source link, recommended action, and status. NOT drama — sourced and precise only.

items.title: Claude Code 1.4 returns empty filesystem-mcp results on non-C: drives (Windows 11)
items.what: Tool calls to filesystem-mcp returned empty results when the configured allow-list path was on a drive other than C:. Affected agents that pointed filesystem-mcp at D: / E: development drives.
items.recommendedAction: Pin Claude Code to 1.3.x or upgrade to 1.4.1+ (released May 6). Workaround for 1.4.x: move project to C: or symlink.

items.title: OpenAI Apps SDK rate limits reduced 50% across all tiers
items.what: Per-app rate limits on the Apps SDK dropped from previous values. Affected apps with bursty usage patterns; many hit 429s within hours of the change.
items.recommendedAction: Add request-rate awareness to your Apps SDK app. Implement exponential backoff. For high-volume apps, contact OpenAI for tier upgrade. Monitor changelog for further adjustments.

items.title: AutoGen enters maintenance mode — Microsoft Agent Framework is the successor
items.what: Microsoft confirmed AutoGen will receive only security and bug fixes going forward. New feature development moves to Microsoft Agent Framework (Python + .NET). Migration deadline for AutoGen 1.x: end of 2026.
items.recommendedAction: New projects: start on Microsoft Agent Framework. Existing AutoGen 1.x projects: plan migration. AutoGen 2.x patterns mostly map to Agent Framework with abstractions.

items.title: MCP Spec 2.0 RFC published — auth and streaming improvements
items.what: Anthropic published the RFC for MCP Spec 2.0. Key changes proposed: refined auth model (OAuth flows), streaming-first transport, multi-tenant primitives. Backwards-compat with 1.x for the foreseeable future; clients and servers can adopt 2.0 features incrementally.
items.recommendedAction: Read the RFC. No urgent action — 1.x stays supported. New servers may want to plan for 2.0 auth shapes.

items.title: Google publishes A2A protocol v0.4 spec
items.what: A2A v0.4 adds agent-discovery primitives, capability advertisements, and a clearer message-envelope format. Compatible with v0.3 with deprecation warnings.
items.recommendedAction: Multi-agent systems on A2A: read the v0.4 spec, plan migration. Single-agent systems: irrelevant.
